iterations.hypothesis: Generalized N-listing pairwise arb auto-discovering (exchange_id, security_id) pairs from market data. Entry threshold 8 bps to cover combined taker fees (~6.8 bps). Staleness guard 500ms to tolerate Lighter's 200ms latency.
iterations.analysis: The strategy is now finding and trading. It auto-discovered 3 listings: (1,1), (1,28), and (2,1). The active pair was (1,28) vs (2,1) — these are DIFFERENT securities (security_id=28 on Hyperliquid vs security_id=1 on Lighter). True cross-exchange arb would be (1,1) vs (2,1) — same security_id on different exchanges. The 8 bps threshold is too high relative to actual spread opportunities between same-security pairs, so only 4 fills occurred over a 20hr window. The cross-asset trade was profitable by accident but is not a reliable arb.
iterations.next_action: Filter to same-security pairs only (sid_a == sid_b) to focus on true cross-exchange arb between (1,1) and (2,1). Reduce entry_threshold_bps to 6.5 bps (just above 6.8 bps total fee, accounting for the 200ms Lighter latency risk). Reduce close_threshold to 1 bps to take PnL sooner.
